title: Sri Sri Nookambika Ammavari Temple
tagline: Sri Sri Nookambika Ammavari Temple — spiritual town of Andhra Pradesh

heroImage.alt: Sri Sri Nookambika Ammavari Temple, Andhra Pradesh

overview.short: Sri Sri Nookambika Ammavari Temple or Sri Nookallamma Ammavaari Devasthanamu, is a temple located in Gavarapalem in the municipality of Anakapalli near the southeastern coast of In
overview.description: Sri Sri Nookambika Ammavari Temple or Sri Nookallamma Ammavaari Devasthanamu, is a temple located in Gavarapalem in the municipality of Anakapalli near the southeastern coast of India. The temple houses the goddess Nookambika. The King who ruled this area - Sri Kakarlapudi Appala Raju Payakarao, built this temple to Kakatambika, a family goddess. She would later be called Nookambika or Nookalamma.

topPlaces.name: Narsipatnam revenue division
topPlaces.description: Narsipatnam revenue division (or Narsipatnam division) is a revenue division in the Anakapalli district of the Indian state of Andhra Pradesh. It is one of the three revenue divisions in the district which consists of twelve mandals under its administration.

topPlaces.name: Paderu revenue division
topPlaces.description: Paderu revenue division (or Paderu division) is an revenue division in the Alluri Sitharama Raju district in the Indian state of Andhra Pradesh. It is one and only revenue divisions in the district which consists of eleven mandals under its administration.

topPlaces.name: Anakapalli revenue division
topPlaces.description: Anakapalli revenue division is an administrative division in the Anakapalli district of the Indian state of Andhra Pradesh. It is one of the three revenue divisions in the district which consists of twelve mandals under its administration.

topPlaces.name: Anakapalli mandal
topPlaces.description: Anakapalli mandal is one of the mandals in located in Anakapalli district of the state of Andhra Pradesh, India. It is administered under Anakapalli revenue division and its headquarters are located at Anakapalli.

topPlaces.name: Anakapalli
topPlaces.description: Anakapalli (Telugu pronunciation: [ana'kaːpalːi]) is a suburb of Visakhapatnam city, located within the jurisdiction of the Greater Visakhapatnam Municipal Corporation. It contains India's second largest jaggery market.

topPlaces.name: Bowluvada
topPlaces.description: Bowluvada (Telugu: బౌలువాడ, Telugu pronunciation: [bawluwaːɖa]) is a census town in Anakapalli mandal of Anakapalli district in the Indian state of Andhra Pradesh. It lies 27 km west of Visakhapatnam.

topPlaces.name: Golagam
topPlaces.description: Golagam is a village in Denkada mandal of Vizianagaram district of the Indian state of Andhra Pradesh. It is located at a distance of 1 km from gurla and at a distance of 1 km from vizayanagaram.

topPlaces.name: Thummapala
topPlaces.description: Thummapala is a village in Anakapalli district of the Indian state of Andhra Pradesh. It is located in Anakapalle Mandal of Anakapalle revenue division.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Sri Sri Nookambika Ammavari Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Sri Sri Nookambika Ammavari Temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Sri Sri Nookambika Ammavari Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Visakhapatnam Airport (23 km) and the nearest railway station is Visakhapatnam Junction (31 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Sri Sri Nookambika Ammavari Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Sri Sri Nookambika Ammavari Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Sri Sri Nookambika Ammavari Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Sri Sri Nookambika Ammavari Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Sri Sri Nookambika Ammavari Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Narsipatnam revenue division, Paderu revenue division, Anakapalli revenue division, Anakapalli mandal, Anakapalli.
